ab: la’i cikuy cu ga maki Pyahaw sali myan, m’was biru lga, mosa cami maki Rato, siy cami aki b’nux maci la.
ch: 小時候我們家住在碧候，上學以後，就搬到了羅東，一直就住在都市裡了。
en: When we were young, our family lived in Pyahaw. After I started school, we moved to Luodong and have been living in the city ever since.

ab: m’was cu hopa pwasan biru lga, mnwah cu tkba utux ppwasan na gaga knxan atayan, aring kya waya mu ba’un atayan na Taywan ga piyux ci ini ptna ku gaga knxan naha.
ch: 上了大學以後，我修了一堂關於原住民文化的課程，瞭解到臺灣原住民族有各式各樣豐富的文化。
en: After I went to college, I took a course on aboriginal culture and learned that the aboriginal people in Taiwan have various and rich cultures.

ab: nanu yaca ku kawas mhngi m’was ’bagan hani ga, cami suyan mu likuy mwah myan alang mita bkis, smoya myan tkba gaga knxan myan nanak.
ch: 因此，今年暑假時，我和哥哥回到部落訪問老人家，想要瞭解自己族群的文化。
en: So this summer, my brother and I went back to our tribe to visit the elderly, hoping to understand the culture of our own ethnic group.

ab: mbkis ga mhenas spat pgan kawas la, ana ga yani ci tiyu pgan kawas texan.
ch: 老人家已經八十歲了，但是看起來還像六十歲。
en: The old man is already eighty years old, but he still looks like a middle-aged man.

ab: kyalun mu heya maha pisa ke ba’un nya kmayan, hyuci ci kmayan ke Tayal, kba kmayan ke Truku, ke Tewan ru ke Nipun.
ch: 我問他會哪些語言，他的泰雅語很流利，也會說太魯閣語、閩南話和日本話。
en: I asked him which languages he knew, and he was fluent in Tayal, as well as Truku, Hokkien and Japanese.

ab: suruw nya ga waya mu ba’un, aring la’i cikuy lga tkba ke Nipun, nerin nya ga Truku, maki beh ptyawan nya ga piyux ba’un nya c’oli Tewan, nanu yaca ku piyux ke ba’un nya kmayan.
ch: 後來我才知道，他從小時候就學日本話，太太是太魯閣族人，他在工作上認識許多閩南人，所以他會說這麼多語言。
en: I asked him to find out that he learned Japanese when he was a child, his wife is an Truku, and he knows many Hokkien at work, so he can speak so many languages.

ab: trang cami pkkayan naga, piyux ci yaw waya skayan na bkis, yaw nku knba’an mluyak na bnkesan.
ch: 我們訪談的時候，老人家談論了許多關於祖先的狩獵傳統知識。
en: During our interview, the old man talked about a lot of traditional knowledge about hunting.

ab: ana myan ini ba’i mung kwara, ana ga waya myan galun syasing rengwa ’ba, mosa myan le’un mung loyi.
ch: 雖然我們沒有辦法完全聽懂，但是我們有用手機錄影，可以再仔細聆聽。
en: Although we could’t fully understand it, we could record it on our cell phones and listen to it more.

ab: la’i cu cikuy ga ini mu ba’i gaga knxan mu nanak, cayux mu ci kinang ga atayan.
ch: 小時候我不瞭解自己族群的文化，對於自己是原住民有點不好意思。
en: When I was young, I didn’t understand the culture of my ethnic group, and I was a little embarrassed that I was an aborigine.

ab: piyux waya mu ba’un lga, kinang ga la’i na Tayal, mnanak ta ci, p’asun ci ru strahu ta ci.
ch: 瞭解愈多以後，自己身為泰雅子孫，是很獨特的，也很值得驕傲。
en: The more I know, the more I feel that being a Tayal is unique and something to be proud of.
